# Qubox ${{ github.ref_name }}

Low-latency remote desktop for creators and gamers.

## Install (recommended)

Download the **desktop app** for your OS from the assets below:

| Platform | Installer |
|----------|-----------|
| **Windows** | `Qubox_*_x64-setup.exe` (NSIS) or `.msi` |
| **macOS** | `.dmg` or `.app` |
| **Linux** | `.AppImage`, `.deb`, or `.rpm` |

1. Install **Qubox** on the machine you want to control (host) **and** the machine you connect from (client).
2. Open the app → create/sign in to [Qubox Cloud](https://qubox.app) → **Enroll this device** with a code from the dashboard.
3. On the host: start hosting. On the client: pick the host and connect.

Cloud signaling: `wss://signal.qubox.app/ws` (default).

## Power users / CLI

Each release also includes raw binaries under `bin/`:

| Binary | Role |
|--------|------|
| `qubox-host-agent` | Host agent (if not using the app) |
| `qubox-client-cli` | CLI client / enroll |
| `qubox-daemon` | Background daemon |
| `qubox-signaling-server` | Self-host open signaling |

**Cloud enroll (CLI):**

```bash
# Linux / macOS
export QUBOX_ACCOUNTS_URL=https://signal.qubox.app
export QUBOX_SERVER=wss://signal.qubox.app/ws
./qubox-client-cli cloud-enroll --code YOUR_CODE
./qubox-host-agent --name "$(hostname)"
```

```powershell
# Windows
$env:QUBOX_ACCOUNTS_URL = "https://signal.qubox.app"
$env:QUBOX_SERVER = "wss://signal.qubox.app/ws"
.\qubox-client-cli.exe cloud-enroll --code YOUR_CODE
.\qubox-host-agent.exe --name $env:COMPUTERNAME
```

## Self-host signaling

Use the public Docker image / `qubox-signaling-server` binary for your own signaling server. Managed friends/access control remains on Qubox Cloud.
